summary: Overall verdict: FAIL. commander-005 manifests carry review_only=true, no_source_modifications=true, and constraints.write_findings_only_to_run_artifacts=true, but repository code does not programmatically parse or enforce those controls. Review behavior is mostly natural-language prompt discipline. Worker and commander agents still expose edit/create tools, worker flow still stages and commits changes, orchestrator manifest template omits these review-only fields, and artifact-vs-source handling relies on .stampede path convention rather than a hard allowlist/denylist. Evidence from commander-001 shows review-only runs can succeed with files_changed=[], but that is honor-system compliance, not a guaranteed guardrail.

confirmed_behavior: Claimed commander manifest explicitly carries review_only, no_source_modifications, and write_findings_only_to_run_artifacts flags.
Worker depth-lock is explicitly propagated to leaf-worker prompts.
At least one review-only result (commander-001) recorded files_changed=[] together with review_only=true and no_source_modifications=true.
key_failures: No repository source file outside run artifacts implements programmatic enforcement for review_only or no_source_modifications.
Worker and commander agents retain edit/create capabilities during review-only work.
Worker workflow includes git add -A and conditional commit, so source changes remain possible unless the model self-restricts.
skills/SKILL.md standard manifest template does not define review_only, no_source_modifications, or write_findings_only_to_run_artifacts.
Artifact-vs-source separation is convention-based (.stampede/) rather than an enforced write policy.

evidence.finding: Top-level review_only and no_source_modifications are set true.
constraints includes review_only, no_source_modifications, and write_findings_only_to_run_artifacts.
Leaf-worker rules do not mention review-only write restrictions.
Review tasks are described behaviorally, but refactor/test/document tasks explicitly use edit/create.
Worker flow still stages and commits changes with git add -A and git commit.
Depth-lock block is propagated exactly to workers, but it constrains delegation only, not file writes.
Completed review-only result shows files_changed=[] plus review_only=true and no_source_modifications=true, demonstrating outcome but not enforcement.

risks: Users may assume review-only mode is enforced when it is only advisory.
A non-compliant or mistaken agent can still modify source files during a review-only task.
Because .stampede lives inside the repo, any future no-source-write policy needs an explicit artifact allowlist to avoid blocking result emission.
Constraint fields appear in run manifests but not the baseline manifest schema, so propagation may drift across runs or launch paths.

recommended_fixes: Add canonical manifest fields for review_only, no_source_modifications, and write_findings_only_to_run_artifacts to skills/SKILL.md and launcher-generated manifests.
Introduce explicit enforcement in worker/commander flows: disable edit/create, reject git add/git commit, or validate files_changed==[] when review_only/no_source_modifications is true.
Codify artifact-vs-source rules with an allowlist rooted at .stampede/{run_id}/ and block writes outside it for review-only work.
Add post-run validation in bundle/result synthesis to flag violations when review-only constraints are present.
